summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Right elbow pain and chest pain.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports waking up with right elbow pain yesterday and describes it as aching and heavy, extending up to the neck. He denies any recent trauma or injury. He reports occasional chest pain, which he describes as coming and going, often associated with the elbow pain. He states he has been mowing the grass recently and feels it contributed to the elbow pain. He reports difficulty stopping pooping and anxiety. He has been taking motrin for the pain, but it doesn't fully relieve it. He denies shortness of breath. He is currently taking norvasc 2.5mg and metformin 500mg.

Findings
Physical examination reveals a slight murmur with cardiac auscultation. The patient reports chest pain and reports pain radiating to the neck. The patient reports elbow pain and arm pain.

Assessment
The patient's presentation is concerning for possible cardiac chest pain, potentially exacerbated by musculoskeletal pain in the left arm. He has a history of hypertension and diabetes mellitus.

Plan
Order an EKG and aspirin. Administer nitroglycerin as needed. Order shoulder radiographs. Continue metformin 500mg and norvasc 2.5mg. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks if symptoms persist. If the EKG reveals concerning findings, we will consult with cardiology and consider transfer to the hospital for further evaluation.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Left arm pain.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Zachary Wood is a 45-year-old right-hand-dominant male who presents to the clinic today for the evaluation of left arm pain. The onset of his pain began yesterday when he woke up with pain. He denies any specific injury. The patient locates his pain from his left elbow up all the way to his neck. He describes his pain as an achiness. The patient has been taking Motrin for pain, which does not provide him with relief. He also reports intermittent chest pain that radiates up his neck. He notes that he had to stop cutting the grass the other day because of chest pain. The patient denies any shortness of breath.

For his hypertension, the patient states that his blood pressure has been around 125/72 recently. He is still taking 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

Regarding his type 2 diabetes, his last hemoglobin A1c was 7.2. He takes metformin 500 mg. The patient states that he does not check his blood glucose levels regularly.

MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient reports that he has a past medical history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes.

MEDICATIONS

Patient reports that he currently takes metformin 500 mg and Norvasc 2.5 mg.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Reports chest pain.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath.
Musculoskeletal: Reports left elbow, arm, and neck pain.

VITALS

The patient's blood pressure today is 150/90.

PHYSICAL EXAM

CV: Slight murmur detected, not heard before.

ASSESSMENT

Left arm pain.
Type 2 diabetes.
Hypertension.

PLAN

Regarding his left arm pain, I have explained to him that his symptoms are likely a mild heart attack. I have recommended that we obtain an EKG for further evaluation. I have also recommended that the patient take aspirin and nitroglycerin for pain. The patient will follow up with me once the EKG results are available for review and further discussion.

In regards to his diabetes, the patient will continue the 500 mg of metformin for now.

For his hypertension, the patient will continue the 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

Chief Complaint
Right elbow pain and chest pain.

The patient reports waking up with right elbow pain yesterday and describes it as aching and heavy, extending up to the neck. He denies any recent trauma or injury. sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Order an EKG and aspirin. Administer nitroglycerin as needed. Order shoulder radiographs. Continue metformin 500mg and norvasc 2.5mg. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks if symptoms persist. If the EKG reveals concerning findings, we will consult with cardiology and consider transfer to the hospital for further evaluation.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports chest pain and reports pain radiating to the neck. The patient reports elbow pain and arm pain.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are patient-reported symptoms and pain descriptions, which belong in the HPI. Listing them under Findings could mislead a reader into interpreting them as exam findings rather than history.

section_miscategorizations.text: He is currently taking norvasc 2.5mg and metformin 500mg.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Current medication state is allowed in HPI per the template, but many readers expect a separate medication list or placement with other medical history. This is a mild organizational issue, not clearly misleading, so it is only a minor concern and still acceptable in HPI.
